subject: LU-2109 llog: Defer closes of log handles under processing

messages.message:
Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

I think OSP should not be involved in this at all - this is responsibility of llog to track records and decide when to destroy llog and when to stop processing.
see the comment by  26/Nov/12 9:02 PM

Patch Set 1:

How about moving the llog_exist() check to llog_cat_process_cb()?

Patch Set 1:

I'd prefer to look at a local copy of llh_count which literally says how many records we should process. anything wrong with this approach ?

Patch Set 1:

As you mentioned in the Jira comment, it is not obvious about how to deal with concurrent additions.  We could do this only for "full" (lgh_last_idx == 64747) logs, but from llog's point of view how does it know the "non-full" case is safe?  From osp's point of view, "non-full" is safe because it knows that the newly added records won't be cancelled before it has processed them; llog can't assume this, I think.

Patch Set 1:

rwe just do not need to apply this until llog is full (last_idx = ...).   osp does not control when and which plain llog to use - it's all under llog's control, nor it control size of llog.
another case is when llog left from the previous boot, but in this case catalog logic does know that plain is not supposed to be reused, so it's safe again.

Patch Set 1:

Oops, the assertion should be "rc != LLOG_DEL_RECORD".

Patch Set 1:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/10809/

Patch Set 1: Verified

Build Successful 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/10809/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 1:

Autotest has started the test process

Patch Set 1:

the issue happens when some records ahead of the current position are cancelled already.
IOW, when this is not the first pass.

log_cancel_rec() destroys llog only if lgh_last_idx==MAX.


when we enter llog_process_thread() we have the following cases:
1) lgh_last_idx == MAX
    no new records are possible, so using a copy of llh_count is safe
2)  lgh_last_idx < MAX
   a) llog is in use, new records are coming
      this is the first scan, as catalog code do not re-use llogs after reboot
      no cancelled records ahead are possible
   b) llog is from previous boot, no new records
      log_cancel_record() won't destroy loghandle
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 2: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(1 inline comment)

it is good technically but I propose to keep just single llog_close() at the end if !llog_exists

Patch Set 2: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(2 inline comments)

as Mike said, the direction is good. it'd be good to think more about robust llog_close().

Patch Set 1:

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.3/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/9b3927e6-3a04-11e2-b2e6-52540035b04c).  Ran 22 tests.  1 tests failed: sanity.
Patch Set 2: (2 inline comments)

Patch Set 2: (2 inline comments)

I think it's very important to comment the changes well, given how much this has taken already.

Patch Set 2:

I was thinking of another approach here: llog_process_thread() could drop LLOG_F_ZAP_WHEN_EMPTY to prevent llog removal, restore once the llog is processed and destroy immediately if llh_count has already got to 1 by that time.

Patch Set 2: (1 inline comment)

Patch Set 2:

Clearing the ZAP_WHEN_EMPTY flag would be nice.  A small concern is that closing handles in llog_process_thread() is not generally as good as closing at the same level of corresponding llog_cat_id2handle() calls.  Again, to be really neat and concurrency-safe, we would need reference counting.  But for now I think either lgh_cur_idx and ZAP_WHEN_EMPTY method is fine; the former needs a bit fewer lines of code (or blame my laziness to implement both).
Uploaded patch set 3.

Patch Set 3: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

i'm not sure how the patch makes sure no loghandle is leaked? (unless we agree this is a short term solution to help LLNL sooner)

Patch Set 3: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

let's review http://review.whamcloud.com/#change,4745 way at first, if it is good then go with it
Patch Set 3: Abandoned

commit.subject: LU-2109 llog: Defer closes of log handles under processing
commit.message:
LU-2109 llog: Defer closes of log handles under processing

When an OSP is processing a plain log using llog_process_thread(), the
log may be destroyed during a record cancellation, in which case the
log handle will be freed in osp_sync_process_queues().  This causes
trouble for llog_process_thread(), as the llh_bitmap[] freed together
with the log handle is needed to guide processing.  This patch detects
busy log handles in llog_cat_cancel_records() via non-zero lgh_cur_idx
values and defers closes of busy log handles to llog_cat_process_cb().

Change-Id: I7af9e44d593e5d3e84ad1ed9f9872c8ae2f885c1
Signed-off-by: Li Wei <wei.g.li@intel.com>
